items.question: Write the PMF of Bernoulli(p) and state E[X] and Var(X).
When is Poisson(λ) a good approximation to Binomial(n, p)?
items.answer: When n is large, p is small, and λ ≈ np, because Binomial counts rare independent events; then P(X=k) ≈ e^{−λ} λ^k / k!. Rule of thumb: n large (e.g. n≥50) and p small with np moderate.

items.question: Relate categorical cross-entropy to the Categorical distribution.
items.answer: For true class y and predicted probabilities π, loss = −log π_y equals negative log-likelihood of a Categorical(π) model. Minimizing average cross-entropy maximizes likelihood of observed labels under softmax parameterization.

items.question: Why can Gaussian Naive Bayes produce poor probabilities on skewed features?
items.answer: It assumes each feature is Normal within each class. Skew, bounds, or multimodal features violate that; fitted μ and σ miss tail behavior, so class-conditional PDFs and posteriors can be miscalibrated despite decent accuracy.

items.question: Explain why P(X = x) = 0 for a continuous Gaussian X but f(x) can be positive.
items.answer: For continuous variables, probability is defined on intervals via ∫ f(x) dx. A single point has measure zero, so P(X=x)=0 even when density f(x)>0. Density is not probability.
